General Admission Steps.val: Admission Application Form must be filled online by following the instructions.
Applications received through post or by hand will not be accepted/entertained.
The online system assigns a unique Application Reference Number (ARN) to each applicant, which must be noted for later use.

Applicant will confirm that information entered in the Admission Application Form is correct. In case of incorrect information, the University has all the rights to cancel the application/admission without any fee refund.
Applicant will deposit the Application Fee in the designated Bank. Fee is non-refundable/adjustable.
Upon payment of the Application Processing Fee (and additional Admission Test Fee), the applicant will print his/her ADMIT CARD bearing the applicant’s TEST ROLL NUMBER.
The applicant should then visit the test campus on the specified date and time as mentioned on the Admit card.
Admission of a student automatically stands cancelled if he or she fails in the required exam whose result was not available at the time of admission.

General Admission Notes.val: Admission Test is held at all campuses of the University (Karachi, Lahore, Islamabad, and Peshawar).
GRE (subject test) can apply for admission to graduate programs, i.e. MS, MBA or PhD.
SAT examination can apply for admission to the undergraduate programs, i.e. BBA or BS.
The Institution code for receiving SAT scores is 4575.
The Institution code for receiving GRE scores is 7386.
Foreign nationals must submit a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the Ministry of Education, Government of Pakistan at the time of admission.

Rs. 3000 is payable by all applicants including those who are seeking admission on the basis of NTS (NAT or GAT) test. If an applicant wants to take more than one Admission Test, then an Additional Fee of Rs. 500 is payable for each additional Admission Test.
Admission policy is merit‑based with no quotas or donation seats.
Foreign nationals need NOC from Ministry of Federal Education and Professional Training.
Provisional admission allowed for candidates awaiting FA/FSc/A‑level results.
Maximum time allowed to complete degree requirements: BS/BBA 6 years, MBA 3 years, MS 4 years, PhD 8 years (as per HEC guidelines).
Double admission (concurrent admission in more than one degree) is not allowed.
Suspension of registration can occur due to disciplinary action, unpaid dues, or failure to complete registration formalities.
Readmission rules: former students may be readmitted to graduate or undergraduate programs after passing an admission test, subject to conditions on prior warnings and credit transfer.

Transfer of students between campuses is permitted only on grounds such as death of a parent, posting of a parent, or marriage of a female student, and not allowed if the student has disciplinary or financial issues.
Semester freeze is allowed for exceptional circumstances with a fee; first two semesters for BS and first semester for MS cannot be frozen.

Scholarship_notes.val: Merit scholarship awarded to top three position holders of each Examination Board and top three in university merit list; covers full tuition for four years, continuation requires GPA >= 3.0.
HEC scholarships for MS and PhD cover tuition, stipend and book allowance.
Punjab Educational Endowment Fund (PEEF) offers 42 scholarships to indigent Punjab domicile students for 4-year undergraduate studies, partial tuition covered.
Sindh Government Endowment Board scholarships for Karachi campus, need‑cum‑merit, full tuition, 25 new scholarships per year, 60% rural quota.
Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Education Foundation scholarship for Peshawar campus, need‑cum‑merit, 80% tuition, gender‑balanced.
Baluchistan Government Endowment Board scholarship for Karachi campus, full tuition, renewable annually.
OSAFA (Old Students Association of FAST) provides interest‑free study loans for indigent students; loan covers tuition only and is renewable each semester.
Various community scholarships (Memon, Bohra, INFAQ, Agha Khan, Hindu, etc.) and alumni assistance are available on specific campuses.
Assistantships are offered to students for campus activities with compensation.
Bank loans from PICIC, NIB, Alfalah, Islami and MCB are available.
Study loan repayment starts three months after graduation, to be completed within four years; employers may deduct repayments at source.

Program covers fundamentals of plasma, Debye shielding, plasma parameters, applications, single-particle motion, fluid description, wave propagation, controlled fusion, etc.
Program includes semiconductor fundamentals, device fabrication, thin-film deposition, optoelectronic devices, solar cells, LEDs, lasers, displays.
Program covers imperfections, microstructure, phase diagrams, solidification, mechanical behavior, polymers, biomaterials.
Program introduces nano concepts, quantum effects, surfaces, thin films, material properties, tools, fabrication, nano electronics, nano optics, nano devices.
Program covers pre‑Einstein concepts, Einstein’s postulates, Lorentz transformations, relativistic kinematics, momentum, energy, four‑vectors, and basics of general relativity.

Program includes fundamentals of environment, global climate, spectroscopy, energy balance, transport of pollutants, turbulence, noise, radiation, atmospheric structure, climatology, and measurement techniques.
Program covers quantum computing history, quantum mechanics postulates, qubits, quantum gates, algorithms (Shor, Grover), quantum cryptography (BB84, Ekert), and experimental realizations.
Program expands on quantum information theory, entanglement, quantum error correction, advanced algorithms, and quantum communication protocols.
Program covers Lagrangian and Hamiltonian field theory, canonical quantization, Klein‑Gordon and Dirac fields, gauge invariance, QED, Feynman diagrams, and renormalization.
Program includes laser fundamentals, optical resonators, semiconductor lasers, gas lasers, Q‑switching, mode‑locking, and applications in material processing, medicine, and communications.

name.val: Experimental Techniques in Particle & Nuclear Physics

curriculum_notes.val: Program covers accelerator physics, detector technologies, data acquisition, particle interactions, and hands‑on laboratory work with modern instrumentation.
Program covers fluid kinematics, Euler and Navier‑Stokes equations, irrotational flow, boundary layers, turbulence, wave phenomena, and applications in engineering and biophysics.

Program includes guided wave optics, fiber optics, waveguide modes, dispersion, Gaussian beams, anisotropic media, polarization, electro‑optic and acousto‑optic modulators, lasers, and photonic devices.

Program covers elementary particles, interactions, QED, QCD, weak interactions, relativistic kinematics, symmetries, quantum field theory basics, and modern topics like neutrino oscillations and beyond‑Standard‑Model physics.
Program introduces numerical methods, programming, modeling, Monte Carlo simulations, molecular dynamics, and applications across physics domains.
Program covers clay mineralogy, structure, properties, characterization techniques, and applications in engineering and environmental contexts.

For graduate programs, verification of the last degree by HEC is mandatory.

General Admission Notes.val: SAT score conversion: 1500‑1600 = 50 marks, 1400‑1499 = 45, 1300‑1399 = 40, 1200‑1299 = 35, 1100‑1199 = 30. 100% in SZABIST admission test equals 50 marks.
For master’s programs, 50% score of GMAT is considered.
GAT (General) / HAT relevant with minimum 50% score is mandatory for MS programs; GRE General minimum cumulative score 300 is also accepted.
GAT (Subject) minimum 60% (or GRE Subject 640) is required for PhD programs.

General Admission Notes.val: Undergraduate admission requires SAT I minimum 1200 (600 Math) or Lahore School Entrance Exam, 12-13 years schooling, 60% marks in Matric/Intermediate or equivalent, plus interview.
Application form fee: Rs. 6000; processing fee: Rs. 6000 (required with application).
MBA admission requires 16 years education, CGPA ≥2.5; 2-year MBA open to any graduate, 1.5-year MBA requires BBA/BSc/Hons/BS Hons with CGPA ≥2.5.
Executive MBA (2.5 years, 68 credit hours) requires a 4-year degree with CGPA ≥2.5 and 3 years work experience.
MS/MPhil in Economics requires HAT 3 test, Lahore School Admissions Test, prior academic record, interview, and minimum CGPA 3.0.
PhD in Business Administration requires GRE, HAT 2 test, admissions test, research proposal, interview, and minimum CGPA 3.0.
PhD in Economics requires GRE, HAT 3 test, admissions test, research proposal, interview, and minimum CGPA 3.0.

Admission processing schedule: Application forms available from Jan 5 2026; deadlines June 22‑23 2026; entrance tests early July 2026; interviews late July; ID cards issued mid‑August; classes start Aug/Sep 2026.

Scholarship_notes.val: Lahore School of Economics Scholarships 2025-2026 (PDF)
Undergraduate Need Based Scholarship: 50% tuition waiver for eligible students with 75% intermediate/IBCC marks, financial need, and CGPA ≥3.5; reduced to 25% for CGPA 3.49‑3.0; deadline July 15.

Undergraduate Major Merit Scholarship: 50% tuition waiver for students with 85% marks (or 3 A’s) and CGPA ≥3.75; 25% waiver for 80% marks and CGPA ≥3.5; scholarship maintained each term with CGPA thresholds.
Undergraduate Minor Merit Scholarship (MMS I): fee waiver for a 2‑credit course for 1st‑year students with GPA 3.5; not combinable with other scholarships.
Undergraduate Minor Merit Scholarship (MMS II): fee waiver for a 4‑credit course for 2nd‑4th year students with GPA 4.0; can be combined with Major Merit and Need Based scholarships.
Undergraduate Student TA Ship: tuition credit for a 4‑credit course for 2nd‑4th year students with CGPA ≥3.75; additive to other scholarships.
Graduate Need Based Scholarship (MBA): 50% tuition waiver for students with CGPA ≥3.5 in 4‑year Bachelors and prior need‑based scholarship; reduced to 25% for CGPA 3.49‑3.0; deadline July 15.
Graduate Major Merit Scholarship (2‑year MBA): 50% tuition waiver for BBA/BSc (Hons) graduates with CGPA ≥3.75; reduced to 25% for CGPA 3.74‑3.5; maintained each term.
Graduate Major Merit Scholarship (1.5‑year MBA): 50% tuition waiver for graduates with CGPA ≥3.75; 25% waiver for CGPA ≥3.5; same CGPA thresholds as 2‑year program.
Graduate Minor Merit Scholarship (MBA): fee waiver for a 3‑credit course for MBA students with CGPA ≥3.75; additive to major merit and need‑based scholarships.
Graduate Student TA Ship (MBA): tuition credit for a 3‑credit course for MBA students not on 100% scholarship with CGPA ≥3.5 (or 2nd‑year students with CGPA ≥3.5 after first year).
Mahmood Chaudhry Scholarship: 100% tuition scholarship for one 1.5‑year MBA student already on need‑based scholarship who attains the highest CGPA in the first term; awarded for the remaining year.
Executive MBA Major Merit Scholarship: 50% tuition waiver for eligible BBA/BSc (Hons) graduates with CGPA ≥3.75; same reduction rules as other MBA merit scholarships.
Post‑Graduate PhD Merit Scholarship (Economics & Business Administration): 100% tuition waiver for students with CGPA ≥3.0 after MPhil/MS; maintained each term.
Post‑Graduate MPhil/MS Merit Scholarship (Economics & Business Administration): 100% tuition waiver for students with CGPA ≥3.0 after Hons; maintained each term.
Post‑Graduate MPhil Merit Scholarship (Environmental Science & Policy & Development Studies): 100% tuition waiver for students with CGPA ≥3.0; maintained each term.

Special Need-Based Scholarship: eligibility for single‑parent students (25% additional) and students with disabilities (up to 100% waiver); maintenance CGPA 3.0 for single‑parent, 2.5 for disability; cannot be combined with other scholarships.

Scholarship_notes.val: In Pakistan, currently, less than 8% of the 17-23 age group is enrolled in any form of higher education that is amongst the lowest in the world. The estimated population of this age group will be 30 million by the year 2015. Needs based scholarships would be an answer to offset the challenge of financial constraints. Although in the majority of public sector universities the tuition fee is subsidized but it is still unaffordable for most of the students. Since 35 percent of the Pakistani population is living below the poverty line, it is almost impossible get the higher education for this segment of the society. The objective is to provide equal opportunity for the talented students who are at disadvantage being incapable of meeting cost of education at leading higher education institutions. The Student Financial Aid Office at University of Peshawar is here to serve student body and provide financial assistance in the fairest and most efficient way possible. The mission of the Students Financial Aid Office is to provide University Students with the timely delivery of financial assistance while maintaining accountability and proper stewardship of the public, institutional and private funds with which it is entrusted. We are committed to provide courteous service to support the academic mission and goals of the University and its students. This office provides students with an abundance of general financial aid information to assist students in understanding processes and in making informed financial decisions. Prospective students and their parents are especially invited to visit for general information about university costs, the types of financial aid that are available, the financial aid process, and necessary financial aid application requirements to understand eligibility of the students for financial aid. Prime Minister's National ICT Scholarship Program 2014 at Undergraduate Level for I.T and C.S. Ministry of Information Technology announces this wonderful opportunity for the talented students of rural / nonmetropolitan areas of Pakistan to study in top ICT universities of the country. Applicants have either passed or appeared in F.Sc Examination with Mathematics and Physics in 2013 or 2014 as a regular student from a school / college registered with National ICT R&D Fund. Candidates must have obtained 60% or above marks in Matriculation (Science Group) exam. Ehsaas Undergraduate Scholarship Program aims to eliminate unequal access. Prime Minister's Tuition Fee Payment Scheme for Masters / M.S / M.Phil and Ph. D Students of Less Developed Areas pays tuition for students from less developed areas. HEC-Needs Based Scholarship awarded 250 scholarships for the session 2013-14. Prime Minister's National Programme for Provision of Laptops to Talented Students distributes 100,000 laptops. HEC-French Needs Based Scholarship for 6 Pakistani Universities. USAID Scholarship For B.Ed (Hons) Pre-Step provides 15 scholarships. UoP Merit Cum Need Based Scholarship Program awarded to 200 students in session 2013-14. University of Peshawar Merit Scholarships for B. A/B.Sc Position Holders award full fee waiver. University of Peshawar Brother/Sister Concession offers half tuition for siblings. Peshawar University Teachers Contribution Scholarship for Needy and Deserving Students. Workers Welfare Board Directorate of Education Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Scholarship provides RS. 24,000 per annum. Mahvash and Jahangir Siddiqui Foundation and Sajjad Foundation (Singapore) sponsored Summer Program on Economic and Enterprise Development in NUS. Fauji Foundation Excellence Award provides stipends. Shaheen Excellence Award for top position students. FFC Ward of Farmers Scholarships. National Bank of Pakistan Student Loan Scheme provides interest free loans.

Vision: To be a prominent public sector university in the region, recognized for its global perspective, diverse and supportive learning environment, having international reputation in research and creative discovery and emphasis on leadership development. Mission: The University of Peshawar, a future-oriented and unique institution is committed to achieving excellence in the undergraduate and graduate education, research and public service. The University provides superior and comprehensive educational opportunities through doctoral and special professional educational levels. The University contributes to the advancement of society through research, creative activity, scholarly inquiry, and development of knowledge. The University preserves and promote

s arts, benefits the nation’s economy, serves the citizens through public programs and is dedicated to the production of quality human resource for the knowledge-driven development of the country.

Political Science Department Vision: To establish and strengthen relationship of the department with her counterparts in advanced countries. To bring syllabus of the department at par with those of the developed nations. To carve out the discipline in such a manner that can facilitate the establishment of more departments e.g., Department of Local Government and Rural Development, peace and conflict Resolution etc. Mission: To provide for young, responsible and energetic leadership for induction to the different levels of elected institutions; To ensure transformation of the political culture of Pakistan through the encouragement of Public-spirited, honest and patriotic leadership. To encourage the induction of such leadership who can ensure decency and fairness in the political processes of the country. Objectives: To educate the young students of the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa in the discipline of Political Science; To inculcate among them the sense of responsibility towards the state and society; To impart necessary training to the young graduates to enter into the sphere of politics; To enable them to lead nation in a befitting manner to face challenges of the time.

General Admission Steps.val: Apply online via eportal: https://eportal.iub.edu.pk/
Download admission brochure for Spring-2026: https://www.iub.edu.pk/uploads/765/webmaster-web/1767691846-1767685226-spring26-final.pdf
Apply for MPhil/MS/MSc/PhD (Spring 2026): https://www.iub.edu.pk/uploads/2319/admission-ms-date-upto-10-feb/1770115230-mphil-and-phd-add-spring-2026.pdf
Check important dates: https://www.iub.edu.pk/important-dates
Read eligibility criteria: https://www.iub.edu.pk/eligibility-criteria
Step1: Candidates shall register himself / herself on the university Website www.iub.edu.pk by filling the registration form.
Step 2: After completing registration he / she will be able to fill Admission application form.
Step 3: After submission of valid application form the system will generate a challan form, to be printed by the candidate.
Step 4: The applicant shall deposit prescribed admission processing fee on auto generated challan in any nearest HBL branch.

Step 5: If applicant wants to apply in another program / department, he / she will go back to step 2. For every application form candidate will get a new challan and he / she will have to deposit separate application processing fee.
Step 6: If an applicant needs to update his / her information, he / she will login using candidate registration information and password.
Step 7: The Candidate will be able to check the status of his / her application online.

Step 8: If the candidate’s name appears in a merit list, he/she will present himself/herself in person, with in due time, along with all original documents to the admission committee of the respective department and follow the instructions of the committee.

General Admission Notes.val: Admissions are based on merit with 75% seats allocated on merit and 25% reserved for passed‑outs of ADA/ADS/ADC etc.
Age limit: applicants must be under 26 years (24 years for post‑Intermediate); exemptions apply for B.Ed, MBA Executive, spouses and employees of the university, and armed forces personnel.
Self‑Finance seats require payment of Rs. 100,000 per year in addition to regular or self‑support semester fees.
For the College of Pharmacy, self‑finance seats require Rs. 200,000 per year in addition to regular fees.
PhD admission test equivalent to GRE/HAT General with a passing score of 60% (or 60% in HEC‑accredited test).
Admission criteria for foreign students are determined by the University Admission Committee.
Form transfer is allowed under specific conditions such as vacant seats and applicant eligibility, as detailed in the admission regulations.
Fee voucher must be paid by the date mentioned on the voucher; payment can be made via HBL mobile app, online banking, any HBL branch or HBL Connect agents.
